entries.definition:
h–z–z shaking, vibrating, to swing; to walk briskly; (of God)
to bring forth plants, (of barren land) to become alive; rumbling,
quivering, trembling, commotion, movement. Of this root, two
forms occur five times in the Qur’an: هُزِّي huzzī once and اهْتَزَّ
ʾihtazza four times.

هُزِّي huzzī [fem. imper. of v. هَزَّ hazza, trans.] shake, rattle
(19:25) وَهُزِّي إِلَيْكِ بِجِذْعِ النَّخْلَةِ تُسَاقِطْ عَلَيْكِ رُطَبًا جَنِيًّا and shake the trunk
of the palm tree towards you, it will drop ripe dates upon you.

اهْتَزَّ ʾihtazza [v. VIII, intrans.] 1 to shake, to quiver (28:31) وَأَنْ
أَلْقِ عَصَاكَ فَلَمَّا رَآهَا تَهْتَزُّ كَأَنَّهَا جَانٌّ وَلَّىٰ مُدْبِرًا ‘And throw down your
staff!’—when he saw it quivering like a snake/demon, he turned
and ran away 2 to come to life, to stir, to perk up (22:5) وَتَرَى
الْأَرْضَ هَامِدَةً فَإِذَا أَنْزَلْنَا عَلَيْهَا الْمَاءَ اهْتَزَّتْ وَرَبَتْ وَأَنْبَتَتْ مِنْ كُلِّ زَوْجٍ بَهِيجٍ you
perceive the earth lifeless, yet when We send down upon it water,
it stirs and swells and puts forth vegetation of every joyous kind.

h–z–z shaking, vibrating, to swing; to walk briskly; (of God)
to bring forth plants, (of barren land) to become alive; rumbling,
quivering, trembling, commotion, movement. Of this root, two
